title: ranunculus longicaulis subsp. pulchellus
intro: Ranunculus longicaulis subsp. pulchellus, commonly known as a type of wild buttercup, is a flowering plant native to the mountainous regions of Europe and Asia. This plant has been used in traditional medicine to promote health and well-being, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The therapeutic actions of Ranunculus longicaulis subsp. pulchellus are attributed to its bioactive constituents, which include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, and are thought to have anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiviral properties. side_effects: Ranunculus longicaulis subsp. pulchellus can have side effects if used improperly, such as gastrointestinal issues, including nausea and vomiting, due to the plant's toxic compounds called ranunculin and protanemonin. Ingestion of large amounts can also lead to renal failure, as these toxins can cause damage to the kidneys and disrupt normal kidney function. Additionally, improper use of the plant can result in skin irritation, such as redness, itching, and blistering, due to its caustic properties.
